subject: Public Health
title: Handwashing: The Simplest Lifesaver
description: A fast breakdown of Handwashing: The Simplest Lifesaver for builders.

hook.text: Washing hands with soap reduces diarrheal disease by 30% and respiratory infections by 20%.

buildup.text: Soap breaks apart the lipid membranes of bacteria and viruses—physically destroying pathogens.

discovery.text: Five critical moments: before eating, before cooking, after using the toilet, after changing diapers, and after.

twist.text: Only 19% of people worldwide wash hands with soap after using the toilet—the gap is enormous.

climax.text: Handwashing promotion programs are among the most cost-effective health interventions ever measured.

punchline.text: Twenty seconds of soap and water can prevent more deaths than any vaccine.

quiz.question: What percentage of people worldwide wash hands with soap after using the toilet?
